allNearTerm.question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by March 31, 2026?
allNearTerm.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by March 31, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by March 31 at just 0.2%, reflecting stalled U.S.-brokered trilateral talks amid Russia's ongoing spring offensive launched last week, which has seen mutual strikes and front-line advances claimed by both sides. Key sticking points remain unresolved, including territorial concessions in Donbas and Ukraine's NATO aspirations, with Moscow rejecting Kyiv's recent ceasefire offer and pausing negotiations partly due to Middle East escalations diverting attention. Zelenskiy urged sustained pressure on Russia after U.S. talks concluded March 22, while Putin signals no rush without major Ukrainian retreats. With the deadline imminent and no scheduled summits or breakthroughs announced, traders see negligible odds of de-escalation; only a surprise bilateral deal or external diplomatic intervention could shift this in the final days.

allNearTerm.question: Will Atletico Madrid win the 2025–26 La Liga?
allNearTerm.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will win the 2025–26 La Liga.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 La Liga. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to win the league (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 La Liga season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from La Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: La Liga Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 La Liga (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding four-point lead atop the La Liga table after 29 matchdays—73 points from 24 wins, one draw, and four losses, boasting a +50 goal difference—fuels trader consensus at 76.5% implied probability for the Blaugrana to claim the title. Recent Matchday 29 results, including Barcelona's 3-1 victory over Rayo Vallecano, extended their advantage over Real Madrid, who sit on 69 points following a 3-1 Madrid Derby win but struggle with consistency amid a congested schedule. Villarreal (58 points) and Atletico Madrid (57 points) trail far behind, rendering them negligible contenders with nine matchdays remaining, including high-stakes Clásicos that could test Barcelona's momentum. Real Madrid's faint 21.5% chance reflects their pedigree and potential game-in-hand edge, though upsets remain possible in the tight title race.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding four-point lead atop the La Liga table after 29 matchdays—73 points from 24 wins, one draw, and four losses, boasting a +50 goal difference—fuels trader consensus at 76.5% implied probability for the Blaugrana to claim the title. Recent Matchday 29 results, including Barcelona's 3-1 victory over Rayo Vallecano, extended their advantage over Real Madrid, who sit on 69 points following a 3-1 Madrid Derby win but struggle with consistency amid a congested schedule. Villarreal (58 points) and Atletico Madrid (57 points) trail far behind, rendering them negligible contenders with nine matchdays remaining, including high-stakes Clásicos that could test Barcelona's momentum. Real Madrid's faint 21.5% chance reflects their pedigree and potential game-in-hand edge, though upsets remain possible in the tight title race.

allNearTerm.question: Will Aston Villa win the 2025–26 English Premier League?
allNearTerm.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will win the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to win the league (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding nine-point lead atop the Premier League table after 31 matches—bolstered by a +39 goal difference and recent gritty wins like over Brighton—has solidified trader consensus at 87.5% implied probability for the title, despite Manchester City's game in hand and 2-0 Carabao Cup final victory last weekend. City's draw against Nottingham Forest allowed the gap to widen, highlighting Arsenal's defensive resilience and consistent form under Mikel Arteta. With seven fixtures remaining, Arsenal face a kinder run-in against mid-table sides like Everton and Bournemouth, while City navigate tougher tests. Upsets could arise from Arsenal injuries, fixture pile-ups, or City stringing together maximum points from their game in hand versus Crystal Palace and subsequent clashes.

events.title: UEFA Champions League Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal leads trader consensus at 26.5% implied probability after a composed 3-1 aggregate round-of-16 victory over Bayer Leverkusen, positioning them favorably against underdog Sporting CP in the upcoming quarterfinals starting April 7. eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain's dominant 92.5% implied probability in the Ligue 1 title race stems from their commanding position atop the table with 60 points from 26 matches—a mark where every historical precedent has ended in championship glory—edging Lens by one point after the challengers' 27 games but holding a crucial game in hand and superior +36 goal difference. Realistic challenges include a PSG injury crisis, Champions League fatigue triggering slip-ups in remaining fixtures, or Lens mounting an impeccable run to capitalize on any stumbles.

events.title: NBA MVP
events.description: This is a market on predicting the winner of the NBA Most Valuable Player (MVP) award for the 2025–26 NBA regular season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Shai Gilgeous-Alexander the clear NBA MVP frontrunner at 71.5% implied probability, fueled by Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 58-16 record and his league-leading win shares, underscored by recent 40-point outbursts and 55% field-goal efficiency on 31+ PPG stretches. Victor Wembanyama's surge to 20.6% reflects his historic junior-season dominance—24.2 PPG, 11.2 RPG, elite blocks—driving San Antonio Spurs to a close second at 55-18, topping the March 27 Kia MVP Ladder. Luka Doncic (5.1%) leads scoring at 33.7 PPG but Dallas trails, while Nikola Jokic (2%) anchors fourth-place Nuggets with 12.8 RPG and 10.8 APG amid OKC's late-season clinch of No. 1 seed.

allNearTerm.question: GTA VI released before June 2026?
allNearTerm.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source will be official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

events.title: GTA VI released before June 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source will be official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors "No" on GTA VI releasing before June 2026, with a 97.9% implied probability reflecting Rockstar Games' official November 19, 2026 launch date—firmly reiterated in Take-Two Interactive's February 2026 earnings call amid ongoing polish efforts for the open-world blockbuster. This follows multiple delays from the original Fall 2025 window, first to May 2026 and then six months later, prioritizing quality over rushed timelines in line with Rockstar's perfectionist track record seen in past titles like Red Dead Redemption 2. Recent YouTube updates and industry reports show no advancement signals, with marketing set to ramp up this summer. Realistic upsets, like an improbable surprise digital drop, remain negligible given certification hurdles and historical patterns.

allNearTerm.question: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be László Toroczkai?
allNearTerm.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ahead of Hungary's April 12 parliamentary election, trader consensus prices Péter Magyar at 66.5% to succeed Viktor Orbán as prime minister, reflecting Tisza's widening national poll leads amid economic discontent and voter fatigue after 16 years of Fidesz rule. A March 25 Medián poll showed Tisza at 58% among decided voters versus Fidesz's 35%, expanding an 8-point gap from February, bolstered by Magyar's large March 15 Budapest rally drawing tens of thousands. Orbán counters with patriotic assemblies emphasizing security and stability, but polls indicate Tisza's path to a majority in the mixed electoral system. Fringe candidates like László Toroczkai hold negligible odds absent coalition scenarios.

events.title: NBA Western Conference Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 48.5% implied probability to win the NBA Western Conference, bolstered by their 58-16 record atop the standings and status as the first team to clinch a playoff spot, securing home-court advantage through at least the conference semifinals with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving dominance. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 21.9% following a six-game win streak to 55-18, clinching a top-6 seed and chasing the No. 1 position behind Victor Wembanyama's All-Star impact. Denver Nuggets' 11.5% reflects a slide to fourth at 47-28 amid injury concerns for Jamal Murray (ankle, questionable) and Aaron Gordon (hamstring, out), while Lakers hold 6.2% from third-place momentum at 48-26. Recent clinchings and seeding battles define the late-season playoff picture with eight games left.

events.title: NBA Eastern Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Eastern Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Boston Celtics at 37% implied probability to win the Eastern Conference despite trailing the Detroit Pistons' league-best 54-20 record, driven by Jayson Tatum's mid-March return from an Achilles injury that has elevated Boston's already elite net rating and depth for the playoffs. The Cavaliers (19.5%) and Knicks (15.5%) reflect strong recent form and home-court advantages in a tight race for seeds 2-4, while the surging Pistons (12.5%) face skepticism amid potential playoff matchups against battle-tested foes. allNearTerm.question: Will Xander Schauffele win the 2026 Masters tournament?
allNearTerm.description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Augusta National Invitational - Winner
events.description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads trader consensus at 14.5% implied probability thanks to his unmatched Augusta National mastery—green jackets in 2022 and 2024, plus elite irons and par-5 scoring suited to the demanding layout—but the market stays tightly bunched amid Rory McIlroy's defense of his 2025 playoff victory over Justin Rose, tempered by ongoing back injury recovery that prompted withdrawals from Arnold Palmer and skipped tune-ups. Bryson DeChambeau's back-to-back LIV wins and rising Augusta finishes, alongside Jon Rahm's 2023 title and LIV dominance, fuel their 7.5-7.0% shares, while Cameron Young's Players Championship triumph and Matt Fitzpatrick's Valspar win highlight field parity entering Scheffler's Houston Open withdrawal for his second child's birth.

This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

events.title: UEFA Champions League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal leads trader consensus at 26.5% implied probability after a composed 3-1 aggregate round-of-16 victory over Bayer Leverkusen, positioning them favorably against underdog Sporting CP in the upcoming quarterfinals starting April 7. Bayern Munich trails closely at 22.5% following their dominant 10-2 demolition of Atalanta, though a blockbuster clash with Real Madrid—who stunned Manchester City 5-1 aggregate—tempers expectations. Barcelona (16.5%) and PSG (12.5%) impressed with 8-3 routs of Newcastle and Chelsea respectively, but now face gritty Atletico Madrid and Liverpool in tight ties that heighten upset risks. Liverpool sits at 7.5% amid strong 4-1 progress past Galatasaray, underscoring the knockout volatility keeping the field bunched ahead of potential semifinal heavyweights.

allNearTerm.question: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be János Lázár?
allNearTerm.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ahead of Hungary's April 12 parliamentary election, trader consensus prices Péter Magyar at 66.5% to succeed Viktor Orbán as prime minister, reflecting Tisza's widening national poll leads amid economic discontent and voter fatigue after 16 years of Fidesz rule. A March 25 Medián poll showed Tisza at 58% among decided voters versus Fidesz's 35%, expanding an 8-point gap from February, bolstered by Magyar's large March 15 Budapest rally drawing tens of thousands. Orbán counters with patriotic assemblies emphasizing security and stability, but polls indicate Tisza's path to a majority in the mixed electoral system. Fringe candidates like László Toroczkai hold negligible odds absent coalition scenarios.

events.title: Texas Republican Senate Primary Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton at 68.5% to unseat incumbent Senator John Cornyn at 29.5% in the Republican Senate primary runoff on May 26, driven by Paxton's first-round primary win on March 3 with 38% to Cornyn's 31%, forcing the contest after neither reached a majority. Recent polls, including an Impact Research survey from mid-March showing Paxton ahead 53%-37%, reflect his momentum among MAGA-aligned primary voters viewing Cornyn as insufficiently conservative on border security and spending. CPAC's recent endorsement of Paxton, coupled with reports of an imminent Trump backing, have widened the gap, though Cornyn's fundraising superiority and Senate relationships sustain his viability. Low odds for others like Hunt reflect their primary eliminations.

Victor Wembanyama's surge to 20.6% reflects his historic junior-season dominance—24.2 PPG, 11.2 RPG, elite blocks—driving San Antonio Spurs to a close second at 55-18, topping the March 27 Kia MVP Ladder. Luka Doncic (5.1%) leads scoring at 33.7 PPG but Dallas trails, while Nikola Jokic (2%) anchors fourth-place Nuggets with 12.8 RPG and 10.8 APG amid OKC's late-season clinch of No. 1 seed.

allNearTerm.question: Will Liverpool win the 2025–26 Champions League?
allNearTerm.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Champions League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal leads trader consensus at 26.5% implied probability after a composed 3-1 aggregate round-of-16 victory over Bayer Leverkusen, positioning them favorably against underdog Sporting CP in the upcoming quarterfinals starting April 7. Bayern Munich trails closely at 22.5% following their dominant 10-2 demolition of Atalanta, though a blockbuster clash with Real Madrid—who stunned Manchester City 5-1 aggregate—tempers expectations. Barcelona (16.5%) and PSG (12.5%) impressed with 8-3 routs of Newcastle and Chelsea respectively, but now face gritty Atletico Madrid and Liverpool in tight ties that heighten upset risks. Liverpool sits at 7.5% amid strong 4-1 progress past Galatasaray, underscoring the knockout volatility keeping the field bunched ahead of potential semifinal heavyweights.

allNearTerm.question: Will John Cornyn win the 2026 Texas Republican Primary?
allNearTerm.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Texas. 

If no 2026 Texas Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

events.title: Texas Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Texas. 

If no 2026 Texas Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Texas Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton at 68.5% to unseat incumbent Senator John Cornyn at 29.5% in the Republican Senate primary runoff on May 26, driven by Paxton's first-round primary win on March 3 with 38% to Cornyn's 31%, forcing the contest after neither reached a majority. Recent polls, including an Impact Research survey from mid-March showing Paxton ahead 53%-37%, reflect his momentum among MAGA-aligned primary voters viewing Cornyn as insufficiently conservative on border security and spending. CPAC's recent endorsement of Paxton, coupled with reports of an imminent Trump backing, have widened the gap, though Cornyn's fundraising superiority and Senate relationships sustain his viability. Low odds for others like Hunt reflect their primary eliminations.

allNearTerm.question: Will Cameron Young win the 2026 Masters tournament?
allNearTerm.description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Augusta National Invitational - Winner
events.description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads trader consensus at 14.5% implied probability thanks to his unmatched Augusta National mastery—green jackets in 2022 and 2024, plus elite irons and par-5 scoring suited to the demanding layout—but the market stays tightly bunched amid Rory McIlroy's defense of his 2025 playoff victory over Justin Rose, tempered by ongoing back injury recovery that prompted withdrawals from Arnold Palmer and skipped tune-ups. Bryson DeChambeau's back-to-back LIV wins and rising Augusta finishes, alongside Jon Rahm's 2023 title and LIV dominance, fuel their 7.5-7.0% shares, while Cameron Young's Players Championship triumph and Matt Fitzpatrick's Valspar win highlight field parity entering Scheffler's Houston Open withdrawal for his second child's birth.

allNearTerm.question: Will Barcelona win the 2025–26 La Liga?
allNearTerm.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will win the 2025–26 La Liga.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 La Liga. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to win the league (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 La Liga season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from La Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: La Liga Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 La Liga (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding four-point lead atop the La Liga table after 29 matchdays—73 points from 24 wins, one draw, and four losses, boasting a +50 goal difference—fuels trader consensus at 76.5% implied probability for the Blaugrana to claim the title. Recent Matchday 29 results, including Barcelona's 3-1 victory over Rayo Vallecano, extended their advantage over Real Madrid, who sit on 69 points following a 3-1 Madrid Derby win but struggle with consistency amid a congested schedule. Villarreal (58 points) and Atletico Madrid (57 points) trail far behind, rendering them negligible contenders with nine matchdays remaining, including high-stakes Clásicos that could test Barcelona's momentum. Real Madrid's faint 21.5% chance reflects their pedigree and potential game-in-hand edge, though upsets remain possible in the tight title race.

events.title: NBA Western Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Western Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 48.5% implied probability to win the NBA Western Conference, bolstered by their 58-16 record atop the standings and status as the first team to clinch a playoff spot, securing home-court advantage through at least the conference semifinals with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving dominance. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 21.9% following a six-game win streak to 55-18, clinching a top-6 seed and chasing the No. 1 position behind Victor Wembanyama's All-Star impact. Denver Nuggets' 11.5% reflects a slide to fourth at 47-28 amid injury concerns for Jamal Murray (ankle, questionable) and Aaron Gordon (hamstring, out), while Lakers hold 6.2% from third-place momentum at 48-26. Recent clinchings and seeding battles define the late-season playoff picture with eight games left.

Luka Doncic (5.1%) leads scoring at 33.7 PPG but Dallas trails, while Nikola Jokic (2%) anchors fourth-place Nuggets with 12.8 RPG and 10.8 APG amid OKC's late-season clinch of No. 1 seed.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Orlando Magic win the 2025–2026 NBA Eastern Conference Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: NBA Eastern Conference Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Boston Celtics at 37% implied probability to win the Eastern Conference despite trailing the Detroit Pistons' league-best 54-20 record, driven by Jayson Tatum's mid-March return from an Achilles injury that has elevated Boston's already elite net rating and depth for the playoffs. Tatum's seamless reintegration, highlighted by a 109-98 road win over Cleveland on March 8, underscores the Celtics' experience edge over Detroit's young core lacking postseason pedigree. The Cavaliers (19.5%) and Knicks (15.5%) reflect strong recent form and home-court advantages in a tight race for seeds 2-4, while the surging Pistons (12.5%) face skepticism amid potential playoff matchups against battle-tested foes. Play-in contenders like Charlotte trail at lower probabilities matching their standings.

allNearTerm.question: Will Sergio Fajardo win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
allNearTerm.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M narrowly ahead at 49.5% implied probability for Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round win, with Iván Cepeda Castro close at 42% and Paloma Valencia at 37.7%, reflecting a fragmented post-primary field after March 8 legislative elections and party consultas. Historic Pact's congressional plurality bolsters Cepeda, while Valencia's dominant center-right primary victory and Abelardo de la Espriella's independent appeal keep the race tight amid high undecided voters shown in late-March polls like Guarumo-Ecoanalítica, where Cepeda leads first-round voting intention around 37% but trails slightly in some runoffs. Divergence from polling averages highlights trader bets on consolidation; upcoming debates, endorsements, or Petro administration developments could tip battleground voter blocs toward separation before the June 21 runoff if needed.

If no 2026 Texas Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton at 68.5% to unseat incumbent Senator John Cornyn at 29.5% in the Republican Senate primary runoff on May 26, driven by Paxton's first-round primary win on March 3 with 38% to Cornyn's 31%, forcing the contest after neither reached a majority. Recent polls, including an Impact Research survey from mid-March showing Paxton ahead 53%-37%, reflect his momentum among MAGA-aligned primary voters viewing Cornyn as insufficiently conservative on border security and spending. CPAC's recent endorsement of Paxton, coupled with reports of an imminent Trump backing, have widened the gap, though Cornyn's fundraising superiority and Senate relationships sustain his viability. Low odds for others like Hunt reflect their primary eliminations.

This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads trader consensus at 14.5% implied probability thanks to his unmatched Augusta National mastery—green jackets in 2022 and 2024, plus elite irons and par-5 scoring suited to the demanding layout—but the market stays tightly bunched amid Rory McIlroy's defense of his 2025 playoff victory over Justin Rose, tempered by ongoing back injury recovery that prompted withdrawals from Arnold Palmer and skipped tune-ups. Bryson DeChambeau's back-to-back LIV wins and rising Augusta finishes, alongside Jon Rahm's 2023 title and LIV dominance, fuel their 7.5-7.0% shares, while Cameron Young's Players Championship triumph and Matt Fitzpatrick's Valspar win highlight field parity entering Scheffler's Houston Open withdrawal for his second child's birth.

events.title: NBA Western Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Western Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 48.5% implied probability to win the NBA Western Conference, bolstered by their 58-16 record atop the standings and status as the first team to clinch a playoff spot, securing home-court advantage through at least the conference semifinals with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving dominance. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 21.9% following a six-game win streak to 55-18, clinching a top-6 seed and chasing the No. 1 position behind Victor Wembanyama's All-Star impact. Denver Nuggets' 11.5% reflects a slide to fourth at 47-28 amid injury concerns for Jamal Murray (ankle, questionable) and Aaron Gordon (hamstring, out), while Lakers hold 6.2% from third-place momentum at 48-26. Recent clinchings and seeding battles define the late-season playoff picture with eight games left.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Boston Celtics at 37% implied probability to win the Eastern Conference despite trailing the Detroit Pistons' league-best 54-20 record, driven by Jayson Tatum's mid-March return from an Achilles injury that has elevated Boston's already elite net rating and depth for the playoffs. Tatum's seamless reintegration, highlighted by a 109-98 road win over Cleveland on March 8, underscores the Celtics' experience edge over Detroit's young core lacking postseason pedigree. The Cavaliers (19.5%) and Knicks (15.5%) reflect strong recent form and home-court advantages in a tight race for seeds 2-4, while the surging Pistons (12.5%) face skepticism amid potential playoff matchups against battle-tested foes. Play-in contenders like Charlotte trail at lower probabilities matching their standings.

allNearTerm.question: Will Paloma Valencia win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
allNearTerm.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M narrowly ahead at 49.5% implied probability for Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round win, with Iván Cepeda Castro close at 42% and Paloma Valencia at 37.7%, reflecting a fragmented post-primary field after March 8 legislative elections and party consultas. Historic Pact's congressional plurality bolsters Cepeda, while Valencia's dominant center-right primary victory and Abelardo de la Espriella's independent appeal keep the race tight amid high undecided voters shown in late-March polls like Guarumo-Ecoanalítica, where Cepeda leads first-round voting intention around 37% but trails slightly in some runoffs. Divergence from polling averages highlights trader bets on consolidation; upcoming debates, endorsements, or Petro administration developments could tip battleground voter blocs toward separation before the June 21 runoff if needed.

events.title: Serie A League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 Serie A league (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter's commanding six-point lead atop the Serie A table after 30 matchdays—69 points with a +42 goal difference—drives trader consensus at 83.5% implied probability for the Scudetto, bolstered by their league-best record of 22 wins despite a recent 1-1 draw at Fiorentina on March 22 that preserved the gap. AC Milan (63 points) and Napoli (62 points), trailing by seven, share slim 6.2% and 6.3% chances respectively, reflecting their strong recent form but tougher remaining fixtures, including an upcoming head-to-head clash that could clarify the chase. Challengers like Atalanta, Roma, Como, and Juventus languish further back with minimal paths to catch up amid Inter's momentum and superior squad depth.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 48.5% implied probability to win the NBA Western Conference, bolstered by their 58-16 record atop the standings and status as the first team to clinch a playoff spot, securing home-court advantage through at least the conference semifinals with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving dominance. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 21.9% following a six-game win streak to 55-18, clinching a top-6 seed and chasing the No. 1 position behind Victor Wembanyama's All-Star impact. Denver Nuggets' 11.5% reflects a slide to fourth at 47-28 amid injury concerns for Jamal Murray (ankle, questionable) and Aaron Gordon (hamstring, out), while Lakers hold 6.2% from third-place momentum at 48-26. Recent clinchings and seeding battles define the late-season playoff picture with eight games left.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to qualify for the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g. they cannot reach the required number of points to advance from its group or qualify for playoffs), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Which countries qualify?
events.description: This is a market on which teams will qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

eventMetadata.context_description: With 42 nations already secured for the expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup—including hosts Canada, Mexico, and USA alongside powerhouses like Argentina, Brazil, France, Germany, Spain, and first-timers Cape Verde, Curaçao, Jordan, and Uzbekistan—the final six spots hinge on UEFA playoffs and the intercontinental play-off tournament. UEFA semi-finals concluded March 26-27 with Bosnia and Herzegovina and Czechia advancing via penalty shootouts over Wales and Republic of Ireland, respectively, joining Italy, Sweden, Poland, Kosovo, and Denmark/Turkey for March 31 finals: Bosnia vs Italy, Sweden vs Poland, Kosovo vs Türkiye, Czechia vs Denmark. Intercontinental semis saw Bolivia edge Suriname 2-1 and Jamaica defeat New Caledonia 1-0, setting finals against Iraq and DR Congo. Traders eye potential upsets in these high-stakes single-leg deciders amid tight matchups and recent momentum shifts.

events.title: Colombia Presidential Election 1st round winner?
events.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's presidential election first round on May 31, 2026, reflecting his consistent polling lead around 35-37% in recent surveys from Guarumo/Ecoanalítica and Centro Nacional de Consultoría, bolstered by the Pacto Histórico coalition's victory in the March 8 legislative elections despite lacking a congressional majority. Cepeda secured his party's nomination via an October 2025 consulta, maintaining frontrunner status amid President Petro's lame-duck term. Paloma Valencia has surged to 19-22% after topping the right-wing interparty consultation on the same date with over 3 million votes, narrowing the gap slightly in the latest March 28 poll, while Abelardo de la Espriella slips to 15-20%. No candidate nears the 50% threshold for outright victory, setting up a potential runoff on June 21.

allNearTerm.question: Will the Miami Heat win the NBA Eastern Conference Finals?
allNearTerm.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Miami Heat win the 2025–2026 NBA Eastern Conference Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2025–26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals based on the rules of the NBA.

If the 2025-26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals winner is not announced by June 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: NBA Eastern Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Eastern Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Boston Celtics at 37% implied probability to win the Eastern Conference despite trailing the Detroit Pistons' league-best 54-20 record, driven by Jayson Tatum's mid-March return from an Achilles injury that has elevated Boston's already elite net rating and depth for the playoffs. Tatum's seamless reintegration, highlighted by a 109-98 road win over Cleveland on March 8, underscores the Celtics' experience edge over Detroit's young core lacking postseason pedigree. The Cavaliers (19.5%) and Knicks (15.5%) reflect strong recent form and home-court advantages in a tight race for seeds 2-4, while the surging Pistons (12.5%) face skepticism amid potential playoff matchups against battle-tested foes. Play-in contenders like Charlotte trail at lower probabilities matching their standings.

allNearTerm.question: Will Aaron Rai win the 2026 Masters tournament?
allNearTerm.description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads trader consensus at 14.5% implied probability thanks to his unmatched Augusta National mastery—green jackets in 2022 and 2024, plus elite irons and par-5 scoring suited to the demanding layout—but the market stays tightly bunched amid Rory McIlroy's defense of his 2025 playoff victory over Justin Rose, tempered by ongoing back injury recovery that prompted withdrawals from Arnold Palmer and skipped tune-ups. Bryson DeChambeau's back-to-back LIV wins and rising Augusta finishes, alongside Jon Rahm's 2023 title and LIV dominance, fuel their 7.5-7.0% shares, while Cameron Young's Players Championship triumph and Matt Fitzpatrick's Valspar win highlight field parity entering Scheffler's Houston Open withdrawal for his second child's birth.

allNearTerm.question: Will the Oklahoma City Thunder win the NBA Western Conference Finals?
allNearTerm.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2025–2026 NBA Western Conference Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2025–26 NBA Western Conference Finals based on the rules of the NBA.

If the 2025-26 NBA Western Conference Finals winner is not announced by June 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: NBA Western Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Western Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 48.5% implied probability to win the NBA Western Conference, bolstered by their 58-16 record atop the standings and status as the first team to clinch a playoff spot, securing home-court advantage through at least the conference semifinals with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving dominance. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 21.9% following a six-game win streak to 55-18, clinching a top-6 seed and chasing the No. 1 position behind Victor Wembanyama's All-Star impact. Denver Nuggets' 11.5% reflects a slide to fourth at 47-28 amid injury concerns for Jamal Murray (ankle, questionable) and Aaron Gordon (hamstring, out), while Lakers hold 6.2% from third-place momentum at 48-26. Recent clinchings and seeding battles define the late-season playoff picture with eight games left.

allNearTerm.question: Will Vicky Dávila win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
allNearTerm.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

events.title: Colombia Presidential Election
events.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M narrowly ahead at 49.5% implied probability for Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round win, with Iván Cepeda Castro close at 42% and Paloma Valencia at 37.7%, reflecting a fragmented post-primary field after March 8 legislative elections and party consultas. Historic Pact's congressional plurality bolsters Cepeda, while Valencia's dominant center-right primary victory and Abelardo de la Espriella's independent appeal keep the race tight amid high undecided voters shown in late-March polls like Guarumo-Ecoanalítica, where Cepeda leads first-round voting intention around 37% but trails slightly in some runoffs. Divergence from polling averages highlights trader bets on consolidation; upcoming debates, endorsements, or Petro administration developments could tip battleground voter blocs toward separation before the June 21 runoff if needed.

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Which countries qualify?
events.description: This is a market on which teams will qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

eventMetadata.context_description: With 42 nations already secured for the expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup—including hosts Canada, Mexico, and USA alongside powerhouses like Argentina, Brazil, France, Germany, Spain, and first-timers Cape Verde, Curaçao, Jordan, and Uzbekistan—the final six spots hinge on UEFA playoffs and the intercontinental play-off tournament. UEFA semi-finals concluded March 26-27 with Bosnia and Herzegovina and Czechia advancing via penalty shootouts over Wales and Republic of Ireland, respectively, joining Italy, Sweden, Poland, Kosovo, and Denmark/Turkey for March 31 finals: Bosnia vs Italy, Sweden vs Poland, Kosovo vs Türkiye, Czechia vs Denmark. Intercontinental semis saw Bolivia edge Suriname 2-1 and Jamaica defeat New Caledonia 1-0, setting finals against Iraq and DR Congo. Traders eye potential upsets in these high-stakes single-leg deciders amid tight matchups and recent momentum shifts.

events.title: Bundesliga Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 Bundesliga (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich's commanding nine-point lead atop the Bundesliga table after 27 matchdays—70 points from 22 wins, four draws, and one loss, with a staggering +72 goal difference (97 goals scored, 25 conceded)—has solidified trader consensus at nearly 99% implied probability for the title. Their dominant form persists, highlighted by a 4-0 rout of Union Berlin on Matchday 27, a victory in Der Klassiker over second-placed Borussia Dortmund (61 points), and recoveries from a recent draw against Bayer Leverkusen. With seven fixtures remaining against mid-table sides like Freiburg, St. Pauli, and VfB Stuttgart, Bayern's superior squad depth and firepower make a collapse improbable. Realistic challenges would require an unprecedented Bayern injury crisis or total shutdown alongside Dortmund winning out, scenarios historically rare this late in the title race.

Liverpool, fifth on 24% odds, lurks as the primary challenger after a 1-1 draw at Tottenham that day via Szoboszlai's strike, supported by better goal difference and a lighter run-in than fourth-placed Villa (16%), whose defeat exposed defensive frailties amid tighter remaining fixtures for Chelsea further back. Tatum's seamless reintegration, highlighted by a 109-98 road win over Cleveland on March 8, underscores the Celtics' experience edge over Detroit's young core lacking postseason pedigree. The Cavaliers (19.5%) and Knicks (15.5%) reflect strong recent form and home-court advantages in a tight race for seeds 2-4, while the surging Pistons (12.5%) face skepticism amid potential playoff matchups against battle-tested foes. Play-in contenders like Charlotte trail at lower probabilities matching their standings.

allNearTerm.question: Will Juan Carlos Pinzón win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
allNearTerm.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M narrowly ahead at 49.5% implied probability for Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round win, with Iván Cepeda Castro close at 42% and Paloma Valencia at 37.7%, reflecting a fragmented post-primary field after March 8 legislative elections and party consultas. Historic Pact's congressional plurality bolsters Cepeda, while Valencia's dominant center-right primary victory and Abelardo de la Espriella's independent appeal keep the race tight amid high undecided voters shown in late-March polls like Guarumo-Ecoanalítica, where Cepeda leads first-round voting intention around 37% but trails slightly in some runoffs. Divergence from polling averages highlights trader bets on consolidation; upcoming debates, endorsements, or Petro administration developments could tip battleground voter blocs toward separation before the June 21 runoff if needed.

allNearTerm.question: BitBoy convicted?
allNearTerm.description:
BitBoy was recently arrested due to a warrant issued for threatening communications he sent to a Judge. You can read more about that here: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/22059661159362

This market will resolve to "Yes" if BitBoy (Ben Armstrong) is convicted of any charges related to the emails he sent to Judge Kimberly Childs, by March 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Convictions must be for criminal charges.

If the case is completely dropped for whatever reason, due to a plea agreement without an admission of guilt, a dismissal, or any other situation occurs in which no judgement is rendered or will be rendered by the court, this market will resolve to "No".

Once an individual is convicted it qualifies for an immediate "Yes" resolution, regardless of later appeals, etc. This market will resolve based on the first conviction in a qualifying case.

The primary resolution source will be official court records, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: NBA Western Conference Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 48.5% implied probability to win the NBA Western Conference, bolstered by their 58-16 record atop the standings and status as the first team to clinch a playoff spot, securing home-court advantage through at least the conference semifinals with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving dominance. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 21.9% following a six-game win streak to 55-18, clinching a top-6 seed and chasing the No. 1 position behind Victor Wembanyama's All-Star impact. Denver Nuggets' 11.5% reflects a slide to fourth at 47-28 amid injury concerns for Jamal Murray (ankle, questionable) and Aaron Gordon (hamstring, out), while Lakers hold 6.2% from third-place momentum at 48-26. Recent clinchings and seeding battles define the late-season playoff picture with eight games left.

allNearTerm.question: Will Vicky Dávila win the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
allNearTerm.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's presidential election first round on May 31, 2026, reflecting his consistent polling lead around 35-37% in recent surveys from Guarumo/Ecoanalítica and Centro Nacional de Consultoría, bolstered by the Pacto Histórico coalition's victory in the March 8 legislative elections despite lacking a congressional majority. Cepeda secured his party's nomination via an October 2025 consulta, maintaining frontrunner status amid President Petro's lame-duck term. Paloma Valencia has surged to 19-22% after topping the right-wing interparty consultation on the same date with over 3 million votes, narrowing the gap slightly in the latest March 28 poll, while Abelardo de la Espriella slips to 15-20%. No candidate nears the 50% threshold for outright victory, setting up a potential runoff on June 21.

allNearTerm.question: Will Paloma Valencia win the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?

Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's presidential election first round on May 31, 2026, reflecting his consistent polling lead around 35-37% in recent surveys from Guarumo/Ecoanalítica and Centro Nacional de Consultoría, bolstered by the Pacto Histórico coalition's victory in the March 8 legislative elections despite lacking a congressional majority. Cepeda secured his party's nomination via an October 2025 consulta, maintaining frontrunner status amid President Petro's lame-duck term. Paloma Valencia has surged to 19-22% after topping the right-wing interparty consultation on the same date with over 3 million votes, narrowing the gap slightly in the latest March 28 poll, while Abelardo de la Espriella slips to 15-20%. No candidate nears the 50% threshold for outright victory, setting up a potential runoff on June 21.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain's dominant 92.5% implied probability in the Ligue 1 title race stems from their commanding position atop the table with 60 points from 26 matches—a mark where every historical precedent has ended in championship glory—edging Lens by one point after the challengers' 27 games but holding a crucial game in hand and superior +36 goal difference. Recent developments, including PSG's strong response to a March 6 loss against Monaco with subsequent wins like a 4-0 thrashing of Nice, have solidified trader consensus amid Lens' solid form. The Ligue 1 postponement of the April 11 PSG-Lens showdown to May 13, allowing leaders rest between Champions League quarterfinal legs versus Liverpool, further tilts sentiment toward PSG despite controversy. Realistic challenges include a PSG injury crisis, Champions League fatigue triggering slip-ups in remaining fixtures, or Lens mounting an impeccable run to capitalize on any stumbles.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Boston Celtics at 37% implied probability to win the Eastern Conference despite trailing the Detroit Pistons' league-best 54-20 record, driven by Jayson Tatum's mid-March return from an Achilles injury that has elevated Boston's already elite net rating and depth for the playoffs. eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich's commanding nine-point lead atop the Bundesliga table after 27 matchdays—70 points from 22 wins, four draws, and one loss, with a staggering +72 goal difference (97 goals scored, 25 conceded)—has solidified trader consensus at nearly 99% implied probability for the title. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's presidential election first round on May 31, 2026, reflecting his consistent polling lead around 35-37% in recent surveys from Guarumo/Ecoanalítica and Centro Nacional de Consultoría, bolstered by the Pacto Histórico coalition's victory in the March 8 legislative elections despite lacking a congressional majority. Cepeda secured his party's nomination via an October 2025 consulta, maintaining frontrunner status amid President Petro's lame-duck term. Paloma Valencia has surged to 19-22% after topping the right-wing interparty consultation on the same date with over 3 million votes, narrowing the gap slightly in the latest March 28 poll, while Abelardo de la Espriella slips to 15-20%. No candidate nears the 50% threshold for outright victory, setting up a potential runoff on June 21.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding nine-point lead atop the Premier League table after 31 matches—with 70 points and a +39 goal difference—has solidified Manchester City as the heavy trader consensus at 82.5% implied probability for second place, despite City holding a game in hand at 61 points from 30 games. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M narrowly ahead at 49.5% implied probability for Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round win, with Iván Cepeda Castro close at 42% and Paloma Valencia at 37.7%, reflecting a fragmented post-primary field after March 8 legislative elections and party consultas. Divergence from polling averages highlights trader bets on consolidation; upcoming debates, endorsements, or Petro administration developments could tip battleground voter blocs toward separation before the June 21 runoff if needed.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads trader consensus at 14.5% implied probability thanks to his unmatched Augusta National mastery—green jackets in 2022 and 2024, plus elite irons and par-5 scoring suited to the demanding layout—but the market stays tightly bunched amid Rory McIlroy's defense of his 2025 playoff victory over Justin Rose, tempered by ongoing back injury recovery that prompted withdrawals from Arnold Palmer and skipped tune-ups. Bryson DeChambeau's back-to-back LIV wins and rising Augusta finishes, alongside Jon Rahm's 2023 title and LIV dominance, fuel their 7.5-7.0% shares, while Cameron Young's Players Championship triumph and Matt Fitzpatrick's Valspar win highlight field parity entering Scheffler's Houston Open withdrawal for his second child's birth.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 48.5% implied probability to win the NBA Western Conference, bolstered by their 58-16 record atop the standings and status as the first team to clinch a playoff spot, securing home-court advantage through at least the conference semifinals with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving dominance. Recent clinchings and seeding battles define the late-season playoff picture with eight games left.

eventMetadata.context_description: Wolves lead trader consensus for last place in the Premier League at 54.4% implied probability, rooted in their current 20th position with 17 points from 31 games and a league-worst goal difference of -30, three points and two GD behind 19th-placed Burnley (20 points, -28 GD). Both sides languish 9-12 points from safety amid the relegation zone, but Wolves' recent surge—eight points from five matches including victories over Aston Villa and Liverpool under Rob Edwards—has narrowed the gap while highlighting Burnley's persistent struggles (just four wins all season). Teams above like West Ham (29 points), Tottenham (30), and Nottingham Forest (32) have distanced themselves via key six-pointers, such as Forest's 3-0 win at Spurs, leaving the wooden spoon battle a virtual two-horse race with head-to-head clashes like Burnley vs Wolves looming.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's presidential election first round on May 31, 2026, reflecting his consistent polling lead around 35-37% in recent surveys from Guarumo/Ecoanalítica and Centro Nacional de Consultoría, bolstered by the Pacto Histórico coalition's victory in the March 8 legislative elections despite lacking a congressional majority. Cepeda secured his party's nomination via an October 2025 consulta, maintaining frontrunner status amid President Petro's lame-duck term. Paloma Valencia has surged to 19-22% after topping the right-wing interparty consultation on the same date with over 3 million votes, narrowing the gap slightly in the latest March 28 poll, while Abelardo de la Espriella slips to 15-20%. No candidate nears the 50% threshold for outright victory, setting up a potential runoff on June 21.

allNearTerm.question: Will Daniel Quintero win the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
allNearTerm.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's presidential election first round on May 31, 2026, reflecting his consistent polling lead around 35-37% in recent surveys from Guarumo/Ecoanalítica and Centro Nacional de Consultoría, bolstered by the Pacto Histórico coalition's victory in the March 8 legislative elections despite lacking a congressional majority. Cepeda secured his party's nomination via an October 2025 consulta, maintaining frontrunner status amid President Petro's lame-duck term. Paloma Valencia has surged to 19-22% after topping the right-wing interparty consultation on the same date with over 3 million votes, narrowing the gap slightly in the latest March 28 poll, while Abelardo de la Espriella slips to 15-20%. No candidate nears the 50% threshold for outright victory, setting up a potential runoff on June 21.

allNearTerm.question: Will Burnley finish in last place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
allNearTerm.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in last place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes in last place in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish in  last place in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically unable to), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – Last Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in last place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Wolves lead trader consensus for last place in the Premier League at 54.4% implied probability, rooted in their current 20th position with 17 points from 31 games and a league-worst goal difference of -30, three points and two GD behind 19th-placed Burnley (20 points, -28 GD). Both sides languish 9-12 points from safety amid the relegation zone, but Wolves' recent surge—eight points from five matches including victories over Aston Villa and Liverpool under Rob Edwards—has narrowed the gap while highlighting Burnley's persistent struggles (just four wins all season). Teams above like West Ham (29 points), Tottenham (30), and Nottingham Forest (32) have distanced themselves via key six-pointers, such as Forest's 3-0 win at Spurs, leaving the wooden spoon battle a virtual two-horse race with head-to-head clashes like Burnley vs Wolves looming.

events.title: English Premier League – 3rd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 3rd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United's commanding trader consensus at 61% implied probability for third place stems from their solid hold on third in the Premier League table after 31 matches, bolstered by a crucial 3-1 home win over Aston Villa on March 15—Casemiro, Cunha, and Sesko scoring in a match that extended United's unbeaten league streak into 2026 with six wins and one draw in their last seven. Liverpool, fifth on 24% odds, lurks as the primary challenger after a 1-1 draw at Tottenham that day via Szoboszlai's strike, supported by better goal difference and a lighter run-in than fourth-placed Villa (16%), whose defeat exposed defensive frailties amid tighter remaining fixtures for Chelsea further back. Arsenal and Manchester City lead comfortably ahead, focusing the race on the final Champions League spot.
